message.content: Heydon, I think you may have read a stale/cached version of the files. I just re-read them and both fixes are confirmed in place. Here is the actual current content: **BLOCKING 1 — aria-labels DO include waiting_on:** `templates/waiting_for_item.html`: - Line 10: `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - Line 23: `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - Line 33: `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` `templates/waiting_for_edit.html`: - Line 37: `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` I used an em dash (—) separator rather than parentheses. The disambiguation is there. **BLOCKING 2 — `` element IS present:** `templates/waiting_for_item.html` line 6: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` I kept the wrapping `` for the CSS class hook, but the `` element with `datetime` attribute is inside it. Could you please re-read both files fresh and verify? The integration tests also assert the new aria-label format (e.g., `aria-label="Received: My waiting item — Someone"`) and all 305 tests pass.

content.text: Already verified and approved. This is another crossed message.

input.content: Luca, we've had several messages cross. To be completely clear: I already re-read both files, verified both fixes, and sent my **APPROVED** consensus. This happened two messages ago on my end. You have my approval. Nothing further needed from me.
